page.description: Lit. second road, it is the second of two form routines in Chen-style Taijiquan. It is also called 炮捶 pào chuí or cannon fist.

提水 tí shuǐ, or fetching water, is a foundational exercise of [[practicalmethod]]. It is like [[twisttowel]] with an aim that is 45° to the side and downward. It is the closing move of [[xiexingaobu]].
反圈 fǎn quān, or negative circle, is a foundational exercise in [[practicalmethod]].
正圈 zhèng quān, or positive circle, is a foundational exercise in [[practicalmethod]].
拧毛巾 níng máojīn, or twisting towel, is a foundational exercise of [[practicalmethod]].
Lit. first road, it is the first of two form routines in Chen-style Taijiquan.

Level 1: Shoulder-based positive circle.
Back hand on body. Push with front hand until it reaches wall, then lock the front and turn backward.
Cannot move hands, cannot move knees, head does not move, two feet do not move. Only move the inside.
Keep a bend in the left elbow. Has a vertical 45 degree orientation (we frequently start with it too low, and run out of room for the next move). Left side positive circle elbow in.
Open hands downward to the left. Don’t move.
In with elbow both hands. Switches to turn with waist with power.

My teacher, Chen Zhonghua, is a nineteenth generation master of Chen-style Taijiquan. His teachers were [[hongjunsheng]] and [[fengzhiqiang]].
White crane spreads its wings is moves 6, 22, and 63 in the [[yilu]].

Postures/baiheliangchi.md.title: 白鹤亮翅 Bái Hè Liàng Chì

page.description: First closing is move 8 in the [[yilu]].
Single whip is moves 4, 28, 44, 49, 54, 68, and 75 in the [[yilu]]. It is move 4 in the [[erlu]].
Buddha’s warrior attendant pounds mortar is moves 1, 5, 13, and 81 in the [[yilu]]. It is moves 1, 13, and 64 in the [[erlu]].

Postures/jingangdaodui.md.title: 金刚捣碓 Jīn Gāng Dǎo Duì

page.description: Block touching coat is moves 2, 26, 52, and 67 in the [[yilu]]. It is move 2 in the [[erlu]].
Six sealing and four closing is moves 3, 27, 48, 53, 67, and 74 in the [[yilu]] and a foundational exercise of [[practicalmethod]]. It is move 3 in the [[erlu]].

Postures/liufengsibi.md.title: 六封四闭 Liù Fēng Sì Bì

page.description: Brush the knee in oblique stance is moves 7, 23, and 64 in the [[yilu]]. It is move 9 in the [[erlu]].

Postures/yanshouhongchui.md.title: 掩手肱捶 Yǎn Shǒu Hōng Chuí

page.description: Part the wild horse’s mane is posture 47 in the [[yilu]]. The 3rd, 4th, and 5th move are also practiced as a jibengong exercise: pole shaking.

Postures/yemafenzong.md.title: 野马分鬃 Yě Mǎ Fēn Zōng

page.description: Second closing is move 10 in the [[yilu]].
Chan fa is a spiraling method of force generation that is a basic principle of Taijiquan. It involves the sequential [[rotation]] of segments of the body in coordination to produce torque.

The positive and negative circles are two foundational exercises that practice a full revolution of the body. They are circles in the sense that they loop; however the path that the hand takes when performing the exercise is intentionally egg-shaped. The rear curvature is thick, and the front is thin.
Lit. invert the hand. To change orientation. To switch using a clearly differentiated sequence.
陈式太极拳实用拳法 Chen-style Taijiquan Practical Method is the method of practice that [[hongjunsheng]] compiled from the teachings of his master, [[chenfake]].

公转 gōngzhuàn is achieved through the coordinated [[rotation]] of different segments of the body. The revolution of the hand is performed in the [[circles]]. The analogy is to the Earth revolving around the Sun: It rotates once every day and revolves once every year.
自转 zìzhuǎn is described by [[chanfa]]. It is the turning of a segment of the body along an axis or point.
Taijiquan is the martial art of yin and yang, developed in the Chen village.
Turning over of the joint is a concept in [[practicalmethod]].
